metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the material fingerprint reaches `_verdict_manifest` in `transition.py`: either (a) `_verdict_manifest` calls `attest.current_material_fingerprint(ticket_id)` directly, (b) `_completion_precheck` computes it and passes it through, or (c) `verify_completion` returns it in the result dict.
findings.finding: The completion signer's material fingerprint injection into `_verdict_manifest` is structurally underspecified: the plan says the completion signer records the fingerprint 'via the SAME helper plan-review uses', but `_verdict_manifest` in `transition.py` currently has no access to the ticket's `PlanContext` or `material_fingerprint` helper, and the plan does not specify when/how this context is constructed in the verify→close→sign flow.
findings.location: ## What — 'The completion signer ALSO records the plan-review material fingerprint'; AC3; src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282-305
findings.suggested_fix: The simplest approach: have `_verdict_manifest` call `attest.current_material_fingerprint(ticket_id)` (already lazy-imported, already used in `claim_gate_check`) and append `f"material: {fp}"` when non-None. This keeps the change local to `transition.py` and uses the existing single-source helper.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief design note (CLAUDE.md, an inline ADR, or a docstring block in signing.py) capturing the data[kind]-as-routing-hint / manifest[0]-as-authoritative contract and the canonical material field set so future signers can implement the pattern correctly.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural pattern — data[kind] as a routing hint validated against authoritative manifest[0], plus material-fingerprint symmetry between plan-review and completion signers — but mentions no update to any ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design doc.
findings.location: ## Clarification / ## Clarification (review): data[kind] is consumed, not dropped
findings.suggested_fix: Add a module-level docstring to signing.py (or a CLAUDE.md section) stating: (1) SIGNATURE events carry data[kind] as a non-signed routing hint; manifest[0] is always authoritative and data[kind] must match it; (2) the canonical material field set (description/AC/file_impact/children) is defined in one helper and must not be redefined elsewhere.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the validity-on-read code path (file + function) that handles legacy completion sigs (without fingerprint) as 'material-unknown' to the Scope section, so the implementing agent has a concrete target for the legacy-treatment clause of AC3.
findings.finding: The Scope section lists only attest.py, transition.py, and signing.py, but AC3 requires that 'legacy completion sigs without it are treated as material-unknown by validity-on-read' — a behavioral requirement on the validity-on-read code path, which is not mentioned in Scope. An implementing agent has no file/function target for this portion of the AC.
findings.location: Scope section vs. AC3 ('Completion manifest records the material fingerprint (legacy completion sigs without it are treated as material-unknown by validity-on-read)')
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add the file/function responsible for completion-signature validity-on-read (e.g. `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py::_check_completion_validity` or wherever it lives) with a note: 'treat absent fingerprint line as material-unknown'. In AC3, split into two measurable sub-criteria: (a) completion manifest records the fingerprint line; (b) validity-on-read returns material-unknown when the fingerprint line is absent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add test-level ACs (or a testing section) covering at minimum: (a) kind stamped correctly at both sign sites, (b) mismatch between `data["kind"]` and manifest-derived kind is caught/handled, (c) completion manifest fingerprint is recorded and recomputed correctly, (d) legacy completion sig (no fingerprint) is treated as material-unknown, and (e) drift-refresh re-sign preserves kind.
findings.finding: The plan introduces several new testable behaviors — (1) `data["kind"]` stamped at both sign sites and validated against `manifest[0]` at reduce-time, (2) material fingerprint recorded in the completion manifest, (3) legacy completion sigs without a fingerprint treated as material-unknown by validity-on-read, (4) drift-refresh re-sign preserving kind — yet the plan contains no testing section, no test-file references, and no ACs that call for test-level verification of any of these paths, including the changed validity semantic for legacy sigs.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `## Testing` section listing unit tests for `sign_plan_review`, `refresh_attestation`, and `_verdict_manifest` covering: correct kind value in emitted event data, fingerprint field presence/value, the legacy-sig material-unknown branch (pass a manifest without a fingerprint line to the validity-on-read helper and assert material-unknown result), and a kind-mismatch rejection case.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact interface change in `signing.py` needed to carry `kind` through to the event `data` dict (e.g. `sign_manifest(..., kind: str | None = None)` kwarg mixed into `record`), and confirm whether the public MCP/library façades should expose it.
findings.finding: `sign_manifest(ticket_id, manifest)` has no parameter for extra event-data fields, so there is currently no mechanism to inject `data["kind"]` into the persisted SIGNATURE event. The plan says 'kind passthrough if needed' for `signing.py` but leaves the interface change unspecified — the implementer has no clear contract for how call sites pass `kind` through.
findings.location: Scope: `src/rebar/signing.py` (kind passthrough if needed) + call sites in attest.py / transition.py
findings.suggested_fix: Add `kind: str | None = None` as a keyword argument to `sign_manifest`; when non-None, include it in `record` (and return value) but NOT in `_canonical_payload`/`manifest` so it remains unsigned. Update the two internal call sites; leave the public MCP façade unchanged (it never needs to set kind).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether story 114f (which introduces `attestation_kind` and `data['kind']` consumption in `process_signature`) is a blocking prerequisite of this story or a follow-on; if follow-on, update the AC to reflect that `data['kind']` is written-but-not-yet-consumed at merge time.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `data['kind']` is 'consumed, not dropped' by `process_signature` via an `attestation_kind(manifest, data)` helper (story 114f), but this function does not exist anywhere in the codebase and the current `process_signature` at `_processors.py:376` does not extract or persist `kind` from event data at all. The dependency on 114f is unverified and its sequencing relative to this story is unspecified.
findings.location: Clarification (review): data[kind] is consumed, not dropped / src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py:376

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `process_signature` changes (reading `data["kind"]`, routing via `attestation_kind`, storing resolved kind in the signature record) are in scope for this story or deferred to story 114f — the final AC item currently describes 114f behavior as if it already exists.
findings.finding: `attestation_kind(manifest, data)` is referenced in the plan's Clarification as an existing REDUCE-TIME routing function inside `process_signature`, but this function does not exist anywhere in the codebase. The current `process_signature` in `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` does not read `data["kind"]` at all and stores no `kind` field on the signature record.
findings.location: ## Clarification (review): data[kind] is consumed, not dropped — final AC item; src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py:376-398
findings.suggested_fix: Split the final AC into two: (1) 'The signers set `data["kind"]` on the SIGNATURE event data' (this story's scope); (2) 'The reducer routes by `data["kind"]` and stores the resolved kind in the signature record' (story 114f's scope, already planned there). This removes the ambiguity about what `process_signature` does today.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact mechanism for passing `data["kind"]` through `sign_manifest` to the SIGNATURE event data dict — e.g., add an optional `kind: str | None = None` kwarg to `sign_manifest` that is included in the `record` when set, or document an alternative approach.
findings.finding: `sign_manifest` in `signing.py` has no parameter or mechanism to inject `data["kind"]` into the SIGNATURE event's data dict. The plan mentions 'kind passthrough if needed' for `signing.py` but does not specify the exact API change required — leaving the call-site-to-event-data pathway structurally underspecified.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `kind: str | None = None` to `sign_manifest`'s signature; include it in `record` as `"kind": kind` when non-None. `sign_plan_review` passes `kind="plan-review"` and `_verdict_manifest`'s caller passes `kind="completion-verifier"`. This keeps the passthrough minimal and backward-compatible.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) explicitly list 114f as a hard prerequisite/blocker so this story's AC can only be accepted when both land together, or (b) extend this story's scope to include the `process_signature` change that copies `kind` from event data into `state['signature']`, making the AC self-contained.
findings.finding: The current `process_signature` reducer explicitly copies only a fixed key set (`manifest, algorithm, signature, key_id, head_sha, signed_at, author`) into `state['signature']` — a `kind` field in event `data` is silently dropped and never surfaces in reduced state. The plan attributes the consuming behavior to story '114f', but does not state 114f as a hard prerequisite, creating a window where `data['kind']` is written but permanently invisible to any reader of `state['signature']`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies the material fingerprint is computed by the same shared helper used in `sign_plan_review` (e.g. 'no independent field-list definition in `transition.py`; the helper is imported and called directly').
findings.finding: The 'Added (review)' deliverable — 'the completion signer uses the SAME helper as plan-review (single source of the material field set)' — has no covering AC in the main AC list. AC3 only verifies that the fingerprint IS recorded, not that the shared-helper constraint (no field-list redefinition) is enforced.
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] `_verdict_manifest` imports and calls the same `material_fields()` / fingerprint helper as `sign_plan_review`; the field list is not duplicated in `transition.py`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Promote the 'reducer records the resolved kind in the signature record' sub-claim to a dedicated AC in the main AC list, and remove the duplicated 'signers set data[kind]' portion from the clarification-section checklist item.
findings.finding: The inline checklist item at the end of 'Clarification (review)' — 'The signers set data[kind]; the reducer routes by it (validated against manifest[0]) and records the resolved kind in the signature record' — partially duplicates AC1+AC2 ('signers set data[kind]') while also introducing a new verifiable deliverable (the reducer records the resolved kind in the signature record) that is absent from the main AC list.
findings.location: Section 'Clarification (review): data[kind] is consumed, not dropped' / inline checklist item
findings.suggested_fix: Add to main AC list: '- [ ] `process_signature` stores the resolved/derived kind in the signature record (self-describing for downstream consumers).' Then simplify the inline clarification to prose only.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the mechanism by which the fingerprint manifest line is backward-compatible (e.g. verifiers ignore unrecognised manifest lines, or the fingerprint is stored outside the signed payload), and add an explicit rollback path or expand-contract sequence for the manifest-schema change.
findings.finding: AC4 asserts that adding a fingerprint line to the completion manifest does NOT break old signatures, but provides no mechanism or expand-contract sequence explaining how this is achieved. The T4 criterion requires that the remedy (rollback/back-out or expand-contract) be explicit — acknowledgement of compat alone is insufficient.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
